name: lxml
what_it_does: Fast XML and HTML processing library for Python — parses, creates, and transforms XML/HTML using libxml2 and libxslt. lxml features: etree.parse() and etree.fromstring(), XPath 1.0 queries (element.xpath()), XSLT transforms, XMLSchema and RelaxNG validation, HTML parser (lxml.html), CSS selectors (cssselect), element tree manipulation, c14n canonicalization, XML signature support, ObjectifiedElement for dict-like access, and 10-100x faster than stdlib xml.etree.ElementTree. Standard library for processing XML configuration files, SOAP/XML APIs, HTML scraping, and structured document parsing.
use_cases: Agent XML API parsing — tree = etree.fromstring(xml_response); namespaces = {'soap': 'http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/'}; values = tree.xpath('//soap:Body//result/text()', namespaces=namespaces) — parse SOAP XML API response with namespace-aware XPath; agent processes legacy enterprise XML APIs

Agent HTML scraping — from lxml import html; tree = html.fromstring(html_content); prices = tree.xpath('//div[@class="price"]/span/text()') — extract data from HTML with XPath; agent web scraper processes HTML faster than BeautifulSoup; XPath more precise than CSS selectors for complex paths

Agent XML generation — root = etree.Element('agents'); agent = etree.SubElement(root, 'agent'); agent.set('id', '1'); agent.text = 'SearchAgent'; xml_bytes = etree.tostring(root, pretty_print=True, xml_declaration=True, encoding='UTF-8') — generate valid XML for API or config; agent builds XML request bodies

Agent XML validation — schema_doc = etree.parse('schema.xsd'); schema = etree.XMLSchema(schema_doc); result = schema.validate(etree.parse('document.xml')); errors = schema.error_log — validate XML against XSD schema; agent ensures XML documents conform to expected format before processing

Agent XSLT transformation — transform = etree.XSLT(etree.parse('style.xsl')); result = transform(etree.parse('document.xml')); html_output = str(result) — transform XML to HTML/JSON/other format; agent converts legacy XML report format to modern output
not_for: JSON processing — lxml is XML/HTML only; use json module, orjson, or pydantic for JSON
Simple HTML scraping — BeautifulSoup4 is friendlier for HTML; lxml best when XPath precision or speed matters
Untrusted XML — standard XML parsers are vulnerable to XXE attacks; use defusedxml wrapper for untrusted XML input in agent security-sensitive contexts
best_when: Processing XML APIs (SOAP, RSS, Atom, SAML), HTML scraping with XPath precision, XML validation against schemas, or XSLT transforms — lxml is 10-100x faster than Python stdlib XML with full XPath/XSLT support.
avoid_when: You're processing JSON, doing simple HTML scraping (BeautifulSoup4 easier), or receiving untrusted XML (use defusedxml for security).

pricing.notes: lxml is BSD licensed. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits. 10-100x faster than Python stdlib xml.etree. XPath queries compiled and cached. Large XML files (100MB+) parsed incrementally with iterparse().

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: XMLSyntaxError with line number for malformed XML. XPathEvalError for invalid XPath expressions. XMLSchemaValidateError with detailed schema violation messages. UnicodeDecodeError for encoding issues. etree.XMLParser errors show byte offset of parse failure.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Parsing same XML produces identical element tree. XPath queries are deterministic. etree.tostring() is deterministic for same element tree. XSLT transforms are deterministic.
known_agent_gotchas: XXE vulnerability with default parser — etree.parse(untrusted_xml) with default parser allows XML External Entity injection; malicious XML can read local files; agent code processing untrusted XML must use defusedxml: from defusedxml import etree; or etree.XMLParser(resolve_entities=False, no_network=True)

Namespace handling required for namespaced XML — element.xpath('//result') fails if XML uses namespace prefix; must use: element.xpath('//ns:result', namespaces={'ns': 'http://example.com/ns'}); agent code parsing SOAP or namespaced APIs must extract and pass namespace map from document

text vs tail for mixed content — element.text is text before first child; element.tail is text after element (following sibling text);

Hello world!

: p.text='Hello ', b.text='world', b.tail='!'; agent HTML text extraction must concatenate text and tail recursively

iterparse() for large files — etree.parse('huge.xml') loads entire file into memory; for 100MB+ XML use etree.iterparse('huge.xml', events=('end',), tag='record') — processes elements one at a time; agent must call element.clear() after processing each element to free memory

lxml html vs etree parsing differ — etree.fromstring(html_bytes) fails on malformed HTML; lxml.html.fromstring(html_bytes) handles real-world HTML gracefully; agent web scraping must use lxml.html module not etree for HTML content

XPath returns list always — element.xpath('//title/text()') returns list even for single result; agent code doing title = element.xpath('//title/text()') gets list not string; use element.xpath('//title/text()')[0] or element.findtext('title') for single value

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: Default lxml parser vulnerable to XXE attacks from untrusted XML. Always use defusedxml or disable external entity resolution for untrusted input. XML bombs (billion laughs) also possible — use parser limits for untrusted XML in agent systems. Trusted internal XML processing is safe.
